title: dianthus pyrenaicus subsp. pyrenaicus
intro: Dianthus pyrenaicus subsp. pyrenaicus is a subspecies of flowering plant in the carnation family, known for its low-growing, spreading habit and pink or white flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The plant is believed to offer health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and soothing skin irritations. Dianthus pyrenaicus subsp. pyrenaicus is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and topical creams, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
